queries: single anchor attention head asymmetric variance regularization transformer 2025 2026
frozen attention head fixed direction anchor regularizer multi-head variance trigger LLM

raw_responses.title: AnchorFormer: Differentiable Anchor Attention for Efficient Vision Transformer (2025/05)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: anchor tokens learn pivotal information, reduce complexity from O(n^2) to O(mn); anchors over TOKENS, not over heads.

raw_responses.title: Gated Attention with Asymmetric Regularization for Transformer-based Continual Graph Learning (SIGIR 2023)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: asymmetric regularization on gated attention; continual graph learning context; different artefact (graph attention not multi-head).

raw_responses.title: Progress on Attention (Transformer Circuits 2025)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: rare/anchor heads point toward tokens salient by infrequency or separators; head-level anchors but EMPIRICALLY DISCOVERED not architecturally FIXED.

raw_responses.title: A Novel Regularization Method for Transformer Models (2025/04)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: novel regularization in transformers; head-level variance penalty among options.

raw_responses.title: Attention Illuminates LLM Reasoning: Preplan-and-Anchor Rhythm (NeurIPS 2025)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: globally focused heads highlight anchor tokens; two-beat preplan-and-anchor rhythm; anchor TOKENS not anchor HEADS.

raw_responses.title: Understanding Multi-Head Latent Attention (DeepSeek MLA)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: MLA low-rank factorization, decompresses latent vector for K/V; compression-induced regularization effect.

raw_responses.title: A Visual Guide to Attention Variants in Modern LLMs
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: GQA, MQA, MLA, sparse attention variants; symmetric head-grouping schemes.

raw_responses.title: Linear Predictability of Attention Heads (2026/03)
raw_responses.snippet: LLM-side: asymmetric subspace overlap growth; cache full-precision reference heads, reconstruct others — head-level asymmetric reference + reconstruction.
